channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Last chance to claim $1,400 stimulus check from 2021 nears
item.description: Taxpayers who haven’t filed their 2021 tax returns may still be eligible to receive one (or more) stimulus payments sent during the height of the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, KRON4

item.title: Lawyer says Prince Harry was unfairly 'singled out' when stripped of UK security detail
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Prince Harry was treated unfairly when he was stripped of his British security detail, his attorney told appeals court judges Tuesday as he sought to win back his government-funded protection.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Supreme Court clears way for Trump admin to fire some probationary workers
item.description: The Supreme Court sided with the Trump administration Tuesday by enabling officials to fire thousands of federal workers in their probationary period, saying the government employee unions that sued don’t have legal standing.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: New York City congestion pricing is likely to remain through the summer
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York's $9 congestion toll on most drivers entering Manhattan appears likely to remain through the summer and possibly into the fall as a lawsuit seeking to stop the Trump administration from ending the program moves forward.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Democratic-led cities and states push back on threats to cut US school funding over DEI
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Some Democratic-led states and cities are pushing back on a Trump administration threat to cut education funding over diversity, equity and inclusion programs, creating a standoff that could test how far the White House is willing to go to press its demands on the nation’s schools.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Spanberger raises $6.7 million in Virginia governor race
item.description: Former Rep. Abigail Spanberger’s (D-Va.) gubernatorial campaign said Monday it raised $6.7 million in the first quarter of the year. The haul included more than 50,000 donations of $100 or less in the first three months of the year, according to the Spanberger campaign.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Stocks rise worldwide on hopes for tariff negotiations
item.description: The big question remains centered on how long Trump will keep his stiff tariffs on other countries, which would raise prices for U.S. shoppers and slow the economy.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: S&P 500 rises 3.4% as some relief washes through financial markets worldwide
item.description: Stocks are rising on Wall Street and around the world to recover some of the historic losses since last week when President Donald Trump announced sharp increases in taxes on goods imported to the U.S. The S&P 500 was up 3.4% in early trading Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: At least 27 dead, over 160 injured in roof collapse at Dominican Republic nightclub
item.description: At least 27 people died and 160 others were injured in the Dominican capital early Tuesday when the roof collapsed at an iconic nightclub where politicians, athletes and others were attending a merengue concert, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: By MARTÍN ADAMES ALCÁNTARA and DÁNICA COTO Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida teens beat up mall employee dressed as Easter Bunny: Police
item.description: A trio of teenagers was accused of attacking a Florida mall employee in an Easter Bunny costume on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, NewsNation

item.title: Stephen A. Smith 'leaving all doors open' for presidential bid
item.description: Smith has a longstanding history of weighing in on politics, but has been particularly critical of the Democratic Party in the wake of President Trump’s success in the 2024 election, pushing for it to refocus on top issues for voters in last year’s election cycle including immigration and inflation.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least 27 dead, over 160 injured in roof collapse at Dominican Republic nightclub
item.description: At least 27 people died and 160 others were injured in the Dominican capital early Tuesday when the roof collapsed at an iconic nightclub where politicians, athletes and others were attending a merengue concert, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: By MARTÍN ADAMES ALCÁNTARA and DÁNICA COTO Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former Sen. Joe Manchin is working on a memoir. 'Dead Center' is scheduled for September
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former Sen. Joe Manchin has a memoir coming out in September, what the self-styled maverick from West Virginia calls his “declaration of independence from the extremes on both sides.” St.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US withholding water from Mexico to address debt, official says
item.description: The United States is holding back water payments to Mexico in order to send a message, the U.S. International Boundary and Water Commissioner told Border Report.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, WGN 9

item.title: Kentucky watches for surging rivers to recede so widespread cleanup can begin
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — After days of unrelenting downpours swelled rivers to near record levels across Kentucky, residents closely monitored waterways Tuesday for signs they had crested, but freezing temperatures could complicate any cleanup efforts.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and KRISTIN M. HALL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WATCH: Dire wolf puppies howl for the first time in over 10,000 years
item.description: The dire wolf, who has been extinct for over 10,000 years was brought back to life as the world's first de-extinct animal.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: RFK Jr. plans to tell CDC to stop recommending fluoride in drinking water
item.description: The addition of low levels of fluoride to drinking water was long considered one of the greatest public health achievements of the last century.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida biker caught on video in highway drive-by shooting
item.description: A Florida motorist was shot at by a motorcyclist wearing a leather jacket emblazoned with the logo of a notorious biker gang.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ex-member of former Gambia dictator's military unit is on trial in the US for torture
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Opening statements are expected Tuesday in the trial of a man accused of torturing political opponents of Gambia's former military dictator Yahya Jammeh nearly 20 years ago, the latest international trial tied to his regime.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How Philadelphia police draw on personal experiences to respond to mental health crises
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — On a frigid February morning, Vanity Cordero, a Philadelphia police officer, heard a call over the radio for a man threatening to jump from a bridge. The details sounded familiar.
item.dc:creator: CLAUDIA LAUER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexican man convicted of killing his child to be deported after encountering ICE officers in Montana jail
item.description: Carlos Ambriz, who was convicted of deliberate homicide in the death of his child, is in custody after he encountered ICE officers inside the Montana prison where he was serving time.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Scholarships for child care are drying up. Now families are paying the price
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — For parents who need to work but can't afford the steep cost of child care, federally funded scholarships can be a lifeline. Delivered through state child care assistance programs, the scholarships can mean the difference between a parent working full time — or not at all.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal magistrate weighs arguments in Idaho news groups' execution access lawsuit
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — A federal magistrate judge is expected to hear arguments Tuesday morning in a lawsuit brought by three news organizations that say Idaho prison officials are unconstitutionally hiding parts of lethal injection executions from public view.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bridal shops brace for impact as tariffs affecting wedding dress prices
item.description: Most of the world's wedding dresses are made in China and the tariffs are increasing wholesale prices for dresses and fabrics.
item.dc:creator: Ella Wales, NewsNation

item.title: Chief Justice Roberts pauses order to return Maryland dad mistakenly deported to El Salvador
item.description: Chief Justice Roberts agreed to pause a deadline for the Trump administration to return a Maryland man mistakenly deported to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Florida charter captain Brock Horner ordered to have no contact with fisherman after arrest
item.description: A Florida charter captain whose angry exchange with a fisherman went viral has been ordered not to contact the alleged victim, according to court documents.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, NewsNation

item.title: Epic Universe opens a portal to the future of theme parks
item.description: As Universal Orlando Resort gears up for the highly anticipated May 22 opening of Epic Universe, it’s clear the resort is entering a new era - one that could redefine what a theme park can offer families.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, KRON4

item.title: Fyre Festival organizers promise 'successful' Mexico event after officials' concerning advisory
item.description: Fyre Festival 2 organizers are doubling down on the music and entertainment event after Playa del Carmen, Mexico, government officials denied knowledge of the party.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Seven GOP senators sign onto bill to check Trump's trade authority
item.description: The legislation would limit Trump’s ability to impose unilateral tariffs without the approval of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: South Carolina's highest court refuses to stop second firing squad execution
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — South Carolina's highest court on Monday rejected the last major appeal from Mikal Mahdi, who is scheduled to die by firing squad later this week for the ambush killing of an off-duty police officer.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Texas track meet stabbing suspect told Austin Metcalf ‘see what happens’ as tent dispute turned deadly: docs
item.description: The 17-year-old suspect accused of stabbing another teen to death allegedly warned the victim to "see what happens" amid the track meet dispute.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration asks Supreme Court to block return of man wrongly deported to El Salvador
item.description: The Trump administration on Monday asked the Supreme Court to block a court order requiring it to return a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported to a notorious prison in El Salvador. The Justice Department argued in an emergency appeal to the justices that U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Reversible damage found in marathon runners' brains: study
item.description: A 26.2-mile run can do some damage to the brain, a study shows, but the impact is temporary and reversible.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, KRON4

item.title: Seven GOP senators sign onto bill to check Trump's trade authority
item.description: Seven Republican senators, including Chuck Grassley (R-Iowa), the Senate’s president pro tempore, and Mitch McConnell (R-Ky.), the former Senate Republican leader, have signed onto a bipartisan bill that would require Congress to approve President Trump’s steep tariffs on trading partners.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WGN 9

item.title: California town reports third fatality related to rare virus linked to death of Gene Hackman's wife
item.description: Investigators recently blamed the rare virus for the death of Betsy Arakawa, the wife of actor Gene Hackman.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Florida charter captain arrested after viral altercation with young fisherman
item.description: Police in Punta Gorda spoke out about the incident Saturday, saying "this type of behavior will not be tolerated.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WGN 9

item.title: What to know about the severe storms and flash flooding hitting parts of the US
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — Parts of the South and Midwest, still reeling from violent storms, tornadoes and flooding that have killed more than a dozen people, face new flooding and tornado warnings that forecasters said could last for days.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, ABC 4
